evals.expected_output: Claude creates a session directory, invokes codex review to review uncommitted changes, reads the output file, and presents a synthesis report
assertions: Creates a session directory under .ultra/collab/ with codex-review in the name
Uses 'codex review --uncommitted' (not codex exec) for review mode
Captures output with '2>&1 | tee' (codex review has no -o flag)
Uses Read tool to read the output file
Extracts review findings from raw output
Presents a synthesis with Consensus and Divergent Views sections

evals.prompt: /codex-collab opinion 'Should we use a monorepo or polyrepo?'
evals.expected_output: Claude sends the question to Codex via exec mode with sandbox, reads output, and presents both opinions
assertions: Uses 'codex exec' (not codex review) for opinion mode
Does NOT reveal Claude's position in the prompt
Presents both opinions with trade-off analysis

evals.prompt: /codex-collab review --base main with a focus on security
evals.expected_output: Claude uses codex exec for custom security review (since scope flags cannot be combined with custom prompts in codex review)
assertions: Uses codex review or codex exec for the security-focused review
Does NOT use hardcoded model names (uses -m <model> or default)
Reads output with Read tool, not Bash stdout
